summary: Officially documented control for NTFS 8.3 short-name creation. Good for systems that do not need DOS-style short names and want to avoid their overhead, but disabling them can break older software that still expects short names.

setting.name: NTFS 8.3 Short Name Creation
setting.casual_explanation: NTFS can create old-style short file names for compatibility. Disabling them can reduce some filesystem overhead, but some legacy software still expects those short names to exist.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable 8.3 name creation when they want a cleaner modern NTFS behavior and do not need compatibility with older applications that rely on short names.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off on all volumes can reduce compatibility with older software, while leaving the Windows default keeps a more flexible per-volume behavior.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem in fsutil 8dot3name guidance. Local FileSys.admx and FileSys.adml document the same numeric modes on the managed policy side: 0 enables short names on all volumes, 1 disables them on all volumes, 2 uses per-volume behavior and is the default item, and 3 disables them on all data volumes.

allowed_values.label: Enable on all volumes
allowed_values.meaning: NTFS creates 8.3 short names on all volumes.

allowed_values.label: Disable on all volumes
allowed_values.meaning: NTFS does not create 8.3 short names on any volume.

allowed_values.label: Per-volume behavior
allowed_values.meaning: Short-name creation is controlled per volume and this is the documented default mode.

allowed_values.label: Disable on all data volumes
allowed_values.meaning: Short names are created only on the system volume and disabled on data volumes.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation = 1, which matches the documented 'disable on all volumes' mode.

windows_defaults.label: Windows default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the normal 8.3 short-name baseline

states.rationale: Use the documented per-volume default mode.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft and the local policy files describe 2 as the default per-volume mode.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that may still need some compatibility flexibility
avoid_for: Systems that intentionally want short names disabled everywhere

states.rationale: Keep the documented per-volume default behavior.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal 8.3 compatibility baseline.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You explicitly want short-name creation disabled on every volume.
tradeoffs: Some short-name overhead can remain depending on volume settings.

recommended_profiles.label: No short names
intended_for: Users who know they do not need legacy short-name compatibility

states.rationale: Disable 8.3 short-name creation on all volumes.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want short names disabled everywhere and do not rely on older software that needs them.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You still run software or scripts that expect 8.3 paths.
tradeoffs: 8.3 short names stop being created on all volumes.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: fsutil 8dot3name
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem and describes the 0, 1, 2, and 3 modes, with 2 as the default per-volume mode.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft FileSys.admx short-name mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file exposes NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation as an enum-backed short-name policy surface with the same 0 through 3 numeric modes.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft FileSys.adml short-name help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains the meanings of the 0, 1, 2, and 3 modes and shows the per-volume option as the default item.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation = 1 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the exact runtime registry path, value name, and 0/1/2/3 mode meanings for NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation. The app writes one of the documented modes directly.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: When defaultvalue is specified, the registry key, HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem\NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation, is set to the defaultvalue. The DefaultValue can have the following values: 0: Enables 8dot3 name creation for all volumes on the system. 1: Disables 8dot3 name creation for all volumes on the system. 2: Sets 8dot3 name creation on a per volume basis. 3: Disables 8dot3 name creation for all volumes except the system volume.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft Learn directly documents the runtime registry path, value name, and numeric mode table for NtfsDisable8dot3NameCreation.
